title: Acute Chest Pain - Anteroseptal STEMI
chief_complaint: Central chest pain radiating to left arm for 2 hours, associated with shortness of breath and diaphoresis

hidden_data.cbc:
Hemoglobin: 13.5 g/dL
WBC: 8.2 x10⁹/L
Platelets: 250 x10⁹/L
hidden_data.ecg:
Sinus tachycardia 110 bpm
ST elevation 3mm in V1-V4
Reciprocal ST depression in II, III, aVF
hidden_data.cxr:
Mild cardiomegaly
No pulmonary edema
No consolidation

key_learning_points: ECG must be obtained within 10 minutes of chest pain presentation
ST elevation in V1-V4 = anteroseptal = LAD artery occlusion
Check right-sided leads (V4R) if inferior STEMI suspected

patient_responses.pain_onset: It started suddenly about 2 hours ago while watching TV
patient_responses.pain_character: Heavy crushing weight on my chest, goes to left shoulder
patient_responses.smoking: Yes, a pack a day for 30 years
patient_responses.past_history: High blood pressure and sugar diabetes, meds not regular
patient_responses.family_history: My father died of heart attack at 55

hints.troponin_hint: May be normal in first 2-4 hours

feedback.correct_message: Excellent work, Doctor. You correctly identified the anteroseptal STEMI. Recognizing ST elevation in V1-V4 and acting quickly is exactly what saves myocardium.
feedback.incorrect_message: This was an anteroseptal STEMI, not what you diagnosed. The ST elevation in V1-V4 with reciprocal changes in inferior leads is the key finding. Remember: ST elevation = injury pattern that needs immediate reperfusion.
feedback.partially_correct_message: You were on the right track identifying a cardiac cause, but the specific diagnosis is anteroseptal STEMI. The ST elevation in V1-V4 tells us this is an acute myocardial infarction needing immediate PCI.

ecg.comment: This is THE most important test for chest pain. You ordered it promptly - excellent clinical judgment.

troponin.comment: Confirms myocardial necrosis. While it may be normal in the first 2-4 hours, ordering it early is still appropriate.

cxr.comment: Can be deferred. While it helps rule out other causes like aortic dissection, it should not delay reperfusion.

cbc.comment: Does not change acute STEMI management. This can be checked after PCI.

ck_mb.comment: Troponin has largely replaced CK-MB. Ordering both is redundant.

feedback.attending_note: Doctor, you handled this case well. Your rapid ECG order likely saved myocardium. For future chest pain cases: always check right-sided leads if inferior involvement, and remember that the cath lab should be activated the moment you see ST elevation. Keep up the excellent work.
